questions.question: Define a discrete random variable and provide an example of a situation where it would be used. Explain the key characteristics of a discrete random variable.
questions.hint: Consider the number of heads in a coin toss

questions.question: A continuous random variable X has a probability density function f(x) = 2x for 0 ≤ x ≤ 1 and f(x) = 0 elsewhere. Find P(0.2 ≤ X ≤ 0.5) and interpret the result.
questions.hint: Use the definition of probability for a continuous random variable

questions.question: Explain the difference between a discrete uniform distribution and a continuous uniform distribution. Provide an example of a situation where each would be used.
questions.hint: Consider rolling a die versus measuring a person's height
